title: Pseuderanthemum haenkeanum
intro: Pseuderanthemum haenkeanum, also known as 'False Chrysanthemum' or 'Gesneria', is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, for its numerous health benefits, which include treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pseuderanthemum haenkeanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pseuderanthemum haenkeanum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Pseuderanthemum haenkeanum belongs to the family Acanthaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. It is an evergreen shrub native to tropical regions, and its classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Acanthaceae, genus Pseuderanthemum, species P. haenkeanum.
